statement: On the completed closed-trace high block, the full source operator S=E^*E is compact and A-bounded.
operator: S=integral_{0.08}^{0.52} E_u^* E_u du in the A-Hilbert topology

hardyGreenBoundStatus.label: closed-trace Hardy/Green A-bound
hardyGreenBoundStatus.reason: The hardened source-range theorem gives E_u^*E_u <= eta_u A uniformly.

representerContinuityStatus.label: continuous A-Riesz representer family
representerContinuityStatus.reason: The scalar source rows have A-Riesz representers g_{u,k} that vary continuously over the compact source window.

sourceOperatorCompactnessStatus.label: compact A-bounded source operator
sourceOperatorCompactnessStatus.reason: Continuity of u -> g_{u,k} on a compact interval lets Riemann sums approximate E^*E in A-operator norm by finite-rank operators.

compactSourceOperatorStatus.label: compact A-bounded source operator
compactSourceOperatorStatus.reason: The imported source-range Hardy/Green theorem closes the compactness mechanism.

proof: Use the closed-trace Hardy/Green estimate to identify each scalar source row with an A-Riesz representer.
The Green/source formulas make u -> g_{u,k} continuous in the A-Hilbert norm on the compact source window.
The operator E^*E is the Bochner integral of rank-two positive operators built from g_{u,1},g_{u,2}.
Uniform continuity on the compact interval gives norm convergence of Riemann finite-rank sums to E^*E, hence compactness.
